commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a metadata-only auto-reply WARN for substantive private `message_tool_only` finals with no source-reply delivery, plus focused unit, runner, QA scenario coverage, and a docs formatting repair.

PR surface: Source +111, Tests +209, Docs +97. Total +417 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows `message_tool_only` suppresses automatic source delivery, and current docs describe the no-message-tool-call case as private final text with no source reply; the PR adds focused tests for that path.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair target remains; the patch has no blocking findings and should proceed through normal maintainer review, CI, and merge handling.

target.title: fix(auto-reply): warn on substantive private message-tool finals
target.body:
AI-assisted: yes (prepared with Codex; maintainer rewrite by @steipete).

## Summary

- Problem: Under `messages.visibleReplies: "message_tool"`, final assistant text is intentionally kept private unless the model calls `message(action=send)`. When a weak or confused model writes a long user-facing final answer but never calls the message tool, operators currently only see silence.
- What changed: Add a metadata-only WARN for unusually substantive private finals in `message_tool_only`: long text, or multi-sentence text above a conservative size floor. Short private finals and `NO_REPLY` remain quiet because they can be intentional silence.
- What did not change: OpenClaw still does not auto-deliver private final text in `message_tool_only`; the response body is never logged; successful cron/approval side effects do not count as source-reply delivery.

Refs #85714. This is the conservative observability step, not an automatic fallback or default-policy change.

## Implementation Notes

- Uses `rawAssistantText`, not assembled final payload text, so verbose/status/usage metadata cannot trigger the warning.
- Uses the shared `isSilentReplyText` predicate for `NO_REPLY` variants.
- Splits actual source-reply delivery evidence from unrelated successful side effects.
- Renames the helper to `private-message-tool-final` to avoid treating every no-message-tool turn as definitely stranded.
- Includes a tiny docs formatter repair for `docs/tools/slash-commands.md`, which was already failing `check-docs` on the current merge base.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/private-message-tool-final.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts`
- `pnpm format:docs:check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main --parallel-tests "pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/private-message-tool-final.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts"`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: substantive private final text under `message_tool_only` now emits an operator WARN when no source reply was delivered, while short/intentional silence does not warn.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw test checkout on branch `fix/85714-message-tool-silent-final-loss`, rebased on `origin/main` at `100dd794684`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/private-message-tool-final.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts`; autoreview reran the same command via `--parallel-tests`; `pnpm format:docs:check` verified the docs formatter repair.

Evidence after fix: Predicate tests cover long text, multi-sentence text, short private finals, empty text, `NO_REPLY` variants, automatic delivery mode, successful message-tool delivery, and send-policy denial. runReplyAgent tests cover the live suppression branch, message-tool delivery, `NO_REPLY` with metadata payloads, short finals, and unrelated cron side effects.

Observed result after fix: Focused tests passed: 8 predicate tests and 44 runReplyAgent tests. Final autoreview returned clean with no accepted/actionable findings. After rebasing on `100dd794684`, the same focused tests passed again and docs formatting reported `Docs formatting clean (660 files)`.

What was not tested: No live Telegram/Discord channel run in this pass; this PR changes gateway logging/diagnostics only and does not change outbound delivery semantics. Maintainer proof override is appropriate for this diagnostics-only branch because the behavior is fully covered at the runReplyAgent suppression boundary and no external transport delivery semantics changed.
